heading: Assistance for livestock producers
label: 1472 Assistance for livestock producers
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002, and not as part of the Emergency Livestock Feed Assistance Act of 1988 which comprises this subchapter or as part of the Agricultural Act of 1949 which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of the 1949 Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.section 1421 of this title

children.heading: Definition of livestock
children.label: a Definition of livestock
children.text: In this section, the term “livestock” includes elk, reindeer, bison, horses, and deer.

children.heading: Availability of assistance
children.label: b Availability of assistance
children.text: In such amounts as are provided in advance in appropriation Acts, the Secretary of Agriculture may provide assistance to dairy and other livestock producers to cover economic losses incurred by such producers in connection with the production of livestock.
Indemnity payments to livestock producers who incur livestock mortality losses.
Livestock feed assistance to livestock producers affected by shortages of feed.
Compensation for sudden increases in production costs.
Such other assistance, and for such other economic losses, as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: d Limitations
children.text: The Secretary may not use the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation to provide assistance under this section.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section.
